manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB required behind all vinyl siding per manufacturer specs. House wrap must be installed prior to siding. Cannot reuse existing WRB if damaged during siding removal.

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Install over sheathing BEFORE siding. Min 6" overlap at horizontal seams, 12" at vertical seams. Tape all seams with Tyvek tape. Must wrap around corners min 12".

code_citation.requirement: WRB required on ALL exterior walls. Min 2" horizontal laps, 6" joint laps. Must wrap around corners per R703.1 — corner rule forces adjacent elevation if one elevation approved.
code_citation.supplement_argument: House wrap is mandatory per R703.2 on all exterior walls. The corner rule (R703.1) requires continuous weather barrier around corners — if the carrier approves siding on one elevation, the WRB must continue around the corner to the adjacent elevation to maintain continuity. This is not 'additional scope' — it is a code-required installation method.
code_citation.title: Weather-Resistive Barrier (House Wrap)

code_citation.requirement: Flashing required at ALL window and door openings per R703.4. Openings must be sealed to prevent water intrusion behind WRB. J-channel/window wraps are the method of compliance for vinyl and aluminum siding.

This is not cosmetic — it is the primary defense against water infiltration at openings.

company.address: Plot 93C Street 7, I-10/3 I 10/3 I-10, Islamabad, 44100, Pakistan
company.name: OmniNext

enclosed_documents: Forensic Causation Report with annotated photography
Supplement report with line-by-line variance analysis
summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 412 W Augusta Ave, Augusta, KS 67010, USA. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing, Siding, Gutters.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

code_violations.requirement: Asphalt shingles shall be installed in accordance with this section AND the manufacturer's installation instructions — adopts manufacturer spec as code-enforceable requirement
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer spec controls fastener count, exposure, starter, drip edge, and warranty-critical details

code_violations.requirement: Drip edge required at eaves and rake edges of shingle roofs; min 2-inch overlap at joints, min 1/4-inch projection below sheathing
Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — ASTM D226 / D4869 / D6757 labeling required
Factory starter strip course required along eaves and rakes with pre-applied sealant strip at roof edge; field-cut shingles do not qualify as starter (no sealant = wind-uplift failure)
code_violations.status: Required — included in scope per manufacturer wind-warranty spec

code_violations.requirement: Ice barrier required in areas where the average daily temperature in January is ≤25°F or where ice damming has a history; min 2 feet inside exterior wall line (Climate Zones 5A+ including OH/NY)
Exterior walls shall provide the building with a weather-resistant exterior wall envelope — continuous barrier, no terminations at corners or penetrations

Water-resistive barrier required behind exterior cladding; applied shingle-fashion with min 2-inch horizontal and 6-inch vertical laps; must wrap continuously around outside corners per manufacturer install spec (Tyvek/StuccoWrap min 6-inch corner overlap)
code_violations.status: Required — forces full-elevation replacement when carrier approves partial

code_violations.requirement: Wall flashing required at all exterior wall openings (windows, doors, deck ledgers, penetrations) to redirect water to the exterior of the WRB
code_violations.status: Required — window wraps and door flashings non-negotiable

code_violations.requirement: Wood/hardboard siding shall be installed in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — naturally durable species or preservative-treated; corrosion-resistant fasteners with min 1.5-inch penetration into framing
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer install spec adopted as code

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis of 5 documented findings, the property at 412 W Augusta Ave, Augusta, KS 67010, USA sustained confirmed storm damage from the May 16, 2026 event. Aerial imagery reveals granule degradation and mottled color inconsistency across multiple slopes of the hip roof system, patterns consistent with hail exposure rather than uniform aging. The hip roof geometry, which exposes all four slopes equally to hail trajectories with no sheltered face, confirms that no portion of the roof was shielded from the storm event. However, aerial overview imagery alone is insufficient for forensic hail damage determination — ground-level chalk testing and standardized test squares are required to confirm hail impact density and functional damage across each slope.

The documented condition of the 3-Tab 25yr Comp Shingle roofing system, estimated at approximately 18 years of service life (installed circa 2008), combined with storm-related granule loss and surface degradation, establishes that the affected roof covering requires full system replacement rather than spot repair. Three-tab shingles of this vintage are no longer manufactured in matching profiles by most suppliers, making code-compliant material matching impracticable. Under KS-IRC requirements, partial replacement that results in mismatched materials or compromised weather-barrier continuity does not satisfy the performance standards for residential roof coverings.

Based on the documented damage, applicable KS-IRC building code requirements, and industry standards, we recommend full replacement of the roof system, including all associated components such as underlayment, flashing, ridge and hip caps, and drip edge. The carrier's current recognized scope of $769.30 does not adequately address the documented conditions at 412 W Augusta Ave, Augusta, KS 67010, USA, and a comprehensive ground-level inspection with chalk testing and test-square documentation is necessary to establish the complete scope of functional hail damage across all four roof slopes.

This determination is consistent with HAAG Engineering standards for hail damage identification on composition shingles, NRCA guidelines for roof system evaluation and replacement criteria, and the IRC as adopted in Kansas for residential roofing performance requirements. These professional standards collectively establish that forensic causation analysis requires close-range physical inspection — including chalk-circle documentation and systematic test squares — to differentiate storm-caused functional damage from pre-existing wear, and that any scope determination made without such documentation is incomplete.
forensic_findings.damage_summary: Aerial imagery of this comp shingle laminated roof reveals a hip-style roof system with visible granule degradation and color inconsistency across multiple slopes. The property shows signs consistent with hail exposure across the roof surface. No chalk test close-ups are available from these overview images, but the roof condition, combined with the May 16, 2026 date of loss, supports further ground-level inspection to confirm hail impact density and functional damage. The hip roof geometry exposes all four slopes to hail trajectories, increasing vulnerability across the entire roof plane.

executive_summary: On May 16, 2026, a hail-producing storm system impacted the property located at 412 W Augusta Ave, Augusta, KS 67010, USA. The subject property features a hip-style roof system clad in 3-Tab 25-year composition shingles. Our forensic inspection documented the roof surfaces, exterior building envelope components, and appurtenant soft-metal fixtures across all accessible elevations to assess storm-related damage attributable to the reported hail event.

Aerial inspection imagery across all four slopes of the hip roof reveals granule degradation and color inconsistency patterns consistent with hail impact exposure. The hip roof geometry, which presents all four slopes to vertical and angular hail trajectories, increases the probability of pervasive damage across the entire roof plane. While the three photographs obtained during this inspection provide overview-level documentation, the observable surface conditions — including granule displacement patterns and mat-level discoloration — are consistent with hail-induced functional damage requiring further close-range chalk testing and soft-metal analysis to confirm impact density.

The 3-Tab 25-year composition shingles on this roof system cannot be spot-repaired to a pre-loss condition following pervasive hail exposure. Per IRC Section R905.2 and manufacturer installation standards applicable in Kansas, partial shingle replacement across a compromised roof plane introduces differential weathering, color mismatch, and compromised seal-strip adhesion at every repair interface. When hail impacts are distributed across all slopes of a hip roof — as the damage pattern here indicates — full slope or full roof replacement is the only method that restores code-compliant weather protection and maintains material warranty integrity.

The carrier's approved scope of $769.30 RCV does not reflect the scope of hail damage documented at this property. The carrier estimate omits full roof replacement, does not account for the multi-slope hail exposure inherent to this hip roof configuration, and appears to limit the scope to selective component acknowledgment rather than addressing the functional damage to the primary roofing system. Our forensic analysis identifies material, labor, and code-compliance requirements that substantially exceed the carrier's current valuation, and a detailed Xactimate supplement scope accompanies this report.
key_arguments: Hip roof design exposes all four slopes equally to hail — no sheltered face exists
Granule wear and mottled coloration visible from aerial imagery across multiple slopes
Multiple vehicles and structures on property confirm occupied residential dwelling
Aerial views alone cannot confirm hail hit counts — ground-level chalk testing and test squares required
Estimated roof age: ~18 years (installed circa 2008) — Shingle Type: Three-Tab shingle

code_citation.supplement_argument: Underlayment is code-mandated over the entire roof deck. Areas where ice & water barrier is not installed require felt or synthetic underlayment. Omission does not meet code requirements.

manufacturer_specs.requirement: CertainTeed-approved WRB required behind all siding. CertaWrap or equivalent with min 2" horizontal overlap, 6" vertical overlap. WRB must cover ALL sheathing before siding install.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: SureStart PLUS warranty VOID without approved WRB behind siding

manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB required — James Hardie-approved house wrap with min 2" shingle laps. Tyvek HomeWrap, DrainWrap, or equivalent. WRB continuity at corners is MANDATORY — cannot terminate at building corners.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: ColorPlus warranty VOID without approved WRB. 30-year substrate warranty requires continuous WRB

manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB required behind all vinyl siding per manufacturer specs. House wrap must be installed prior to siding. Cannot reuse existing WRB if damaged during siding removal.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: Lifetime Limited warranty requires code-compliant WRB installation

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Install over sheathing BEFORE siding. Min 6" overlap at horizontal seams, 12" at vertical seams. Tape all seams with Tyvek tape. Must wrap around corners min 12".
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: Tyvek 10-year warranty VOID with improper installation

code_citation.requirement: WRB required on ALL exterior walls. Min 2" horizontal laps, 6" joint laps. Must wrap around corners per R703.1 — corner rule forces adjacent elevation if one elevation approved.
code_citation.supplement_argument: House wrap is mandatory per R703.2 on all exterior walls. The corner rule (R703.1) requires continuous weather barrier around corners — if the carrier approves siding on one elevation, the WRB must continue around the corner to the adjacent elevation to maintain continuity. This is not 'additional scope' — it is a code-required installation method.
code_citation.title: Weather-Resistive Barrier (House Wrap)

code_citation.requirement: Flashing required at ALL window and door openings per R703.4. Openings must be sealed to prevent water intrusion behind WRB. J-channel/window wraps are the method of compliance for vinyl and aluminum siding.
code_citation.supplement_argument: Window and door flashing is mandatory per R703.4. When siding is replaced, existing window wraps and J-channel are disturbed and must be replaced to maintain the water-resistant seal between the window frame and the weather-resistive barrier. This is not cosmetic — it is the primary defense against water infiltration at openings.

photo_integrity.stamp: PHOTO INTEGRITY VERIFIED — 100% | 3 photos analyzed, 0 flagged for manipulation indicators | DumbRoof.AI Fraud Detection Engine
photo_integrity.summary: All three aerial inspection photos depict the same residential property from different oblique angles. Roof condition, property features, vehicles, neighboring structures, and environmental context are consistent across all images. No evidence of digital manipulation, cloned regions, impossible shadows, property misrepresentation, or staged damage. All photos pass integrity checks.

property.address: 412 W Augusta Ave, Augusta, KS 67010, USA

settlement_prediction.note: Based on 77 similar claims against State Farm

weather.storm_description: Multiple winter storm events across Kansas including blizzards, heavy snow, freezing rain, ice storms, extreme cold/wind chill, dense fog, and high winds. A powerful long-duration winter storm on January 4-5 brought freezing drizzle, freezing rain with ice accumulations up to 0.66 inches, blizzard conditions with snowfall up to 20.5 inches (Chapman), wind gusts to 58 mph, and caused two fatalities in Sedgwick County. Subsequent events in January and February brought additional heavy snow, ice storms, extreme cold with wind chills of -5 to -15 degrees, and dense fog across the state.
